title: Agentic Assurance Profile adoption declaration
description: Schema for `.agentic-assurance/adoption.yaml` in an adopting repository. Declares the pinned upstream profile (version and full commit SHA), the adopting project, the selected adoption profiles, and optional mappings from profile artifacts to existing repository conventions. Additional top-level keys are permitted for local extensions; local extensions must not silently weaken the pinned upstream profile (PROFILE.md section 5).

upstream.description: The pinned upstream profile. A floating branch must not be the sole normative reference (PROFILE.md section 16).

version.description: Pinned profile version: `unreleased` before the first tagged release, otherwise a release tag `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` or pre-release `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH-rc.N`. The `-dev` suffix is only valid in the central repository's VERSION file, never in an adopter pin.

commit.description: Full 40-character lowercase hexadecimal commit SHA of the pinned upstream profile.

human_owner.description: Named human owner or governing body with the authority defined in PROFILE.md section 3.

profiles.description: Selected adoption profiles (PROFILE.md section 5). Use the smallest applicable set.

adoption_stage.description: Self-declared adoption stage; absent means DRAFT. Stages are self-binding: the validator enforces the declared stage's requirements as errors, so declaring a stage the artifacts do not meet fails validation. DRAFT adds no requirements (placeholders and UNKNOWN classifications are allowed). HUMAN_REVIEWED requires no unfilled REPLACE_WITH_ placeholder anywhere in the adoption file or any loaded register, and a `human_review` block with non-empty `date`, `reviewer`, and `record`. CONFORMANT additionally requires that passed `review_after` dates are treated as errors, that every severity-critical invariant has a decided (non-UNKNOWN) `intent.classification`, and that `human_review.approvals` carries at least one attributable entry (`approver`, `review_url`, `at`).

layout.description: Assurance artifact layout. Absent or `split` (the default): one register file per artifact at the `paths` locations. `lite`: a single `.agentic-assurance/assurance.yaml` (assurance-lite.schema.json) holding purpose, non-goals, an optional system description, and the register sections — valid only with the core profile (archived and every other profile use the split layout). Graduating from lite to split preserves IDs: move each section array into its own register file (for example `assurance/INVARIANTS.yaml`) and drop this key.

human_review.description: Pointer to the most recent completed human review (PROFILE.md section 4.3) and its durable record. Additional keys are permitted for local review conventions.

date.description: Date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the completed human review.

reviewer.description: The human reviewer or reviewing body.

record.description: Repository-relative path to the durable review record.

notes.description: Short summary of the review outcome.

approvals.description: Attributable approvals backing the review; at least one is required at stage CONFORMANT. Each entry names the approver, a durable review URL (for example an approved pull-request review), and when the approval was given. The validator checks shape and non-emptiness only; verifying that the URL really is an approved review by a non-author remains a manual step until future tooling verifies it against the forge API.

approver.description: The approving human, for example a forge handle.

review_url.description: URL of the durable approval record (for example an approved pull-request review). Not dereferenced or verified by the validator.

at.description: When the approval was given (ISO 8601 date or timestamp).

covers.description: Optional list of artifact paths or stable IDs the approval covers.

rule.description: Optional local review rule or policy this approval satisfies.

specification_workflow.description: The existing specification or change workflow reused for material changes (PROFILE.md section 9).

root.description: Repository-relative path to the workflow's root directory or entry document.

paths.description: Mappings from profile artifacts to repository-relative paths. Omitted keys use the default `assurance/` layout. Additional mappings are permitted (see docs/MAPPINGS.md).

components.description: Optional impact-routing map: named components binding repository path globs to the invariant IDs they protect. The pull-request drift check flags a change that touches a component's paths unless the added lines of the assurance-artifact diff reference one of the component's invariant IDs, the pull-request description mentions all of them, or the description carries an explicit no-impact statement (docs/ADOPTION.md section 3.7).

paths.description: Gitwildmatch-style globs (`**/`, `**`, `*`, `?`) matched against repository-relative changed-file paths.

invariants.description: Invariant IDs this component carries. Each must exist in the invariant register (split or lite layout).

tests.description: Test path globs for the component. Informational in this release; not yet checked by the validator.

security.description: Security and disclosure locations. Never put a private tracker URL, token, or sensitive identifier in a public file.

policy.description: Repository-relative path to the security policy.

public_assurance_root.description: Repository-relative root of the public assurance artifacts.

restricted_record.description: Non-actionable label for where the restricted security record lives (for example `external_private_system`).

issue_integration.description: How GitHub Issues integrate with durable profile artifacts (PROFILE.md section 14).

stable_id_required.description: Whether material Issues must reference stable profile IDs.

public_security_issues_allowed.description: Whether potentially exploitable findings may be filed as public Issues. Must be false for public repositories.

closing_requires_artifact_update.description: Whether closing a material Issue requires the related artifact, evidence, defeater, and residual updates to be complete.
